Use Case Guide

Best Construction Software for Subcontractors (2026)

Subs have different needs than GCs. Here's what actually matters — and what you can skip.

✓ Updated April 2026

Subcontractors are caught in an awkward position with software: you need your own business management tools, but you also have to work within whatever platform your GC is using. The right software strategy for a sub is different from a GC's — and most articles miss this distinction.

This guide covers what subcontractors actually need in software and which platforms deliver it at a price that makes sense for sub-scale businesses.

What subcontractors actually need

Subs have distinct software needs that don't always match what GC-focused platforms offer:

  • Bid management: Track incoming bid requests, submitted quotes, win/loss rates, and outstanding bids
  • Time tracking: Accurate crew time by job — critical for knowing whether you're making money
  • Job costing: Compare estimated labor and material costs to actual costs per project
  • Crew scheduling: Manage your crews across multiple concurrent jobs
  • Invoicing: Bill GCs accurately and on time — cash flow is everything for subs
  • Document storage: Store contracts, plans, permits, and lien waivers by job

What most subs don't need: Homeowner client portals, owner selection management, or enterprise RFI workflows. Don't pay for features that serve the GC relationship, not your business.

Top platforms for subcontractors

1. Contractor Foreman — Best budget sub platform

Pricing: Basic $49/mo · Standard $79/mo · Plus $119/mo · Unlimited $299/mo

Contractor Foreman covers everything a sub needs at a price point that makes sense. Time tracking with GPS verification, job costing, estimating/proposals, purchase orders, and document management are all included. For a sub company with 3–20 field workers, the Unlimited plan at $299/mo for all users is exceptional value.

Best for: Specialty subs (framing, concrete, electrical, plumbing) managing multiple crews and needing cost tracking without breaking the budget on software.

2. Jobber — Best for service-style subs

Pricing: Core $69/mo · Connect $169/mo · Grow $349/mo

If you're a sub doing mostly individual service-call style work — repair calls, small TIs, reactive maintenance — Jobber's workflow is designed around exactly that. Rapid quote creation, scheduling, crew dispatch, and invoicing are streamlined for high-volume, shorter-duration jobs.

Best for: Electrical subs, plumbing subs, and HVAC subs doing a mix of service and small project work.

3. Fieldwire — Best field tool for subs on large commercial jobs

Pricing: Free (3 projects) · Pro $19/user/mo · Business $29/user/mo

When you're working as a sub on a large commercial project, you need plan access and task coordination without paying for a full PM platform. Fieldwire solves this: your field team gets current drawings on their tablets, can create tasks tied to plan locations, and manage their own punch list items — all separate from the GC's Procore account.

Best for: Subs on commercial projects who need independent plan management and field coordination tools.

4. JobTread — Best for subs acting as small GCs

Pricing: Basic $99/mo · Standard $199/mo · Pro $349/mo

If you're a sub who sometimes acts as the GC on smaller projects — managing other subs, holding the contract with the owner — JobTread gives you GC-level project management at a reasonable price. The estimating, client portal, and change order tools work for this scenario without requiring Buildertrend's $499/mo commitment.

Try JobTread — For subs who occasionally wear the GC hat. Full PM tools at sub-friendly pricing.

Visit JobTread →

The GC's platform: what you get for free

An important thing many subs miss: most GC platforms give subs free access for the specific jobs they're assigned to.

  • Buildertrend: GCs can add subs at no cost on all plans. You'll see your assigned tasks, job documents, and schedule — no subscription required.
  • Procore: Subs on Procore jobs can access the job they're working on through the GC's account.
  • JobTread: Sub access is included at certain plan tiers for the GC.

This means you might use Fieldwire or Contractor Foreman for your internal business management while accessing Buildertrend or Procore on specific jobs through the GC's invitation — without paying for either platform yourself.

Time tracking: the most important sub feature

For subcontractors, time tracking is the single most valuable software feature. Here's why: labor is typically 30–50% of your cost on a job, and without accurate time tracking, you're estimating your next bid based on guesswork rather than real data.

The discipline of clocking crew time by job, comparing actual hours to estimated hours at job closeout, and using that data to calibrate future bids is what separates profitable subcontractors from those who are always wondering where the money went.

Contractor Foreman, Jobber, and JobTread all include solid mobile time tracking. Make this the first feature you implement on any platform.

Invoicing and cash flow

Cash flow is existential for subcontractors. Slow invoicing, missed lien deadlines, and unclear billing cause more sub business failures than bad work. Your software should make invoicing fast:

  • Generate invoices from completed work in one click
  • Send automated payment reminders
  • Accept credit card and ACH payments online
  • Track lien waiver exchange with GC payments

Jobber has the best payment collection workflow for subs. Contractor Foreman and JobTread are solid. QuickBooks integration is important for any of these — don't use software that doesn't sync with your accounting.

Frequently asked questions

Do I need construction software if I'm a small sub?

At minimum: QuickBooks for accounting + a simple time tracking app. If you're doing more than 10 jobs per year and managing even one or two employees, adding a platform like Contractor Foreman at $49–$79/mo is worth it. The job costing visibility alone typically recovers the cost quickly.

Can I use Buildertrend as a subcontractor?

You don't need to subscribe — GCs add you for free. If you want to use Buildertrend as your primary business management tool (acting as both GC and sub at different times), it's more expensive than most subs need.

What's the best software for a two-person sub crew?

Contractor Foreman Basic at $49/mo or Jobber Core at $69/mo. Both give you enough to manage your jobs professionally without overpaying. Start with time tracking and invoicing, add other features as you grow.
